skill.name: obsidian-theme-designer
skill.description: Design and refine custom Obsidian themes with browser previews, font selection, color palettes, and generated CSS snippets.
skill.summary: Create visual Obsidian theme previews and generate CSS snippets for a vault.
skill.author: XiangyuSu611

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristics, but I found no evidence of prompt injection, credential theft, weak cryptography, or Windows SAM database access. Confirmed risks are legitimate but sensitive workflow steps: reading Obsidian vault files, launching helper scripts, downloading Google Fonts, and installing fonts into user font directories.

medium_findings.title: External Font Download and Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to fetch Google Fonts CSS, extract font URLs, download font files, and run platform-specific install commands. This is a legitimate feature, but it creates network and command-execution risk if performed without user approval or URL validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and network requests are explicitly documented in the skill. The intent is font installation, not exfiltration, so the risk is real but bounded.

medium_findings.title: Vault File Reading and Snippet Writing
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to read Obsidian configuration, installed theme files, snippets, and note content, then write a generated CSS snippet into the vault. This is expected for theme design, but it requires clear user consent because vault notes may contain private information.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access instructions are direct and central to the workflow. They appear legitimate, but they expose private vault content to the agent context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Dependency URLs
low_findings.description: README files link to Claude Code and related plugin repositories. These URLs are normal documentation links and do not indicate hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible dependency references in README files. I found no evidence that these links are used for automated data transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography and Windows SAM findings come from markdown text and ordinary words, including the sample instruction to render the SAME content. No cryptographic operation or SAM database access was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed no crypto APIs, hashes, or SAM paths in the flagged context. The static matches are lexical false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands for Font Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples using curl, grep, cp, and powershell.exe. These commands should only run after the user approves font download and installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and executable. They are not hidden or malicious, but they affect the host environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Writes to Obsidian and Font Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow writes generated CSS snippets and copies font files into user font locations. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it changes local files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write targets are stated directly in the skill. The locations are user-scoped, but the action still requires informed consent.

content.value_statement: Obsidian theme customization is hard when users must guess colors, fonts, and CSS changes. This skill guides visual choices in browser previews and produces a CSS snippet for the selected vault.
actual_capabilities: Reads Obsidian appearance settings, installed themes, snippets, and vault structure.
Shows style directions, color palettes, and font pairings through browser previews.
Supports light mode, dark mode, and side-by-side dual-mode preview flows.
Uses real vault content to preview headings, tables, tags, quotes, links, and code blocks.
Generates Obsidian-compatible CSS snippets with native theme variables.
Provides platform-specific font installation guidance after user approval.
limitations: Requires access to an Obsidian vault folder to tailor previews to real content.
Font download and installation may require network access and local file changes.
Browser previews depend on the referenced visual companion workflow.
Generated CSS may still need adjustment for unusual community themes or plugins.

use_cases.title: Create a Research Vault Theme
use_cases.description: Build an academic visual style with readable typography, clear headings, and dual light and dark palettes.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Personal Knowledge Base
use_cases.description: Compare warm, cool, and neutral directions before committing to a custom snippet.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Bilingual Writing Workspace
use_cases.description: Preview Chinese and English text together to choose fonts that work across languages.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Theme Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a custom Obsidian theme for this vault. Show me visual style options before generating CSS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a guided design flow

prompt_templates.title: Match a Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this screenshot as the visual reference for my Obsidian theme. Keep the result readable for long writing sessions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a website, app, or screenshot to emulate

prompt_templates.title: Build Dual Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dual light and dark Obsidian theme with the same fonts, different accent colors, and a side-by-side preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for light and dark theme support

prompt_templates.title: Tune Typography and CSS
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current Obsidian theme variables, propose better font pairings, and generate a CSS snippet after I approve the preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using existing theme settings

output_examples.input: Design my Obsidian theme for a research vault with Chinese and English notes.
output: A browser preview with academic, minimal, and warm paper style directions.
Font pairing recommendations that show Chinese, English, numbers, tags, and code.
A final Obsidian CSS snippet and clear import steps after approval.

output_examples.input: Make my vault feel like a clean code editor at night.
output: A dark immersive palette with muted backgrounds and high-contrast code blocks.
A sidebar and editor preview covering headings, tables, links, tags, and quotes.
A matching light mode option if the user asks for dual-mode support.

output_examples.input: I like this screenshot. Use it as inspiration for Obsidian.
output: A visual analysis of color temperature, density, typography, and decoration level.
Preview variants that apply the reference style to real vault content.
A refined CSS snippet once the chosen direction is confirmed.

best_practices: Review the browser preview before allowing CSS or font files to be written.
Approve network font downloads only when you trust the source and need local fonts.
Keep an existing theme or snippet backup before enabling the generated CSS.
anti_patterns: Do not run font installation commands without explicit user approval.
Do not use private vault notes in shared screenshots or public examples.
Do not skip the visual preview and generate final CSS from text preferences alone.

faq.question: Does this skill require CSS knowledge?
faq.answer: No. It asks visual preference questions and generates the CSS snippet after the design is approved.

faq.question: Can it design both light and dark themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports light-only, dark-only, and dual-mode previews with shared fonts.

faq.question: Will it read my private notes?
faq.answer: It may read vault content to create realistic previews. Review permissions before running it on sensitive vaults.

faq.question: Does it install fonts automatically?
faq.answer: The workflow includes font download and installation steps, but these should run only after user approval.

faq.question: Can it work with community themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect installed theme variables, but unusual themes may need manual CSS refinement.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
